items.title: 'Everybody is equal at the table': Inside the ACT's Circle Sentencing court

children.content: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

gallery.altText: A person in a dark shirt stands inside an office.

thumbnail.altText: A person in a dark shirt stands inside an office.

items.title: Award-winning artist accused of copying second artwork

children.content: Australian artist Jane Allan, revealed to have previously copied another painter's work, is being accused of a second case of imitation.

image.altText: An artwork featuring a human-like outline.

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

image.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: How a discarded coffee cup helped solve a 24-year murder mystery

children.content: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother a discarded coffee cup on a east coast road trip led to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

gallery.altText: An older man and woman sitting on a bench and smiling.

thumbnail.altText: An older man and woman sitting on a bench and smiling.

items.title: 'Left an enormous hole': Man sentenced after crash killed Canberra father

children.content: A Canberra man has been spared jail after being found guilty of negligent driving causing the death of motorcyclist and father Craig O'Neill in a crash two years ago.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: Seeking equality, Afghan cricketers head to world cup they cannot play in

children.content: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.

image.altText: Benafsha Hashimi, Afghanistan womens cricketer

items.title: Canberra is known for its local shops but some are falling into disrepair

children.content: Local shops are a distinctive feature of Canberra, but while some are thriving, others have fallen into disrepair and offer no sense of community for nearby residents.

gallery.altText: Man wearing a white jumper inside a bakery.

thumbnail.altText: Man wearing a white jumper inside a bakery.

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship reports attack

children.content: The UN pauses its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel reports an attack.

image.altText: A ship in the forground and another moving a long the horizon the background.

items.title: 'An outright lie': Minister challenges Butler on Thriving Kids rollout

children.content: A member of the Thriving Kids advisory panel has voiced concern about how the support program for children with autism and developmental delay is being rolled out.

image.altText: A close up of a kindergartener's hands on a table

items.title: How China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target people in Australia

children.content: The Australian government raises concerns directly with China over the country's new ethnic unity law, which allows Beijing to target people overseas.

10 people stand side by side wearing different ethnic minority outfits.

items.title: Battle lost for Zempilas and WA Liberals in hope of winning war ahead

children.content: Basil Zempilas has had his first brush with the political truism "disunity is death" after MP Jonathan Huston quit the WA Liberal Party, but he's unlikely — at this stage at least — to be the one left in a political grave.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas is speaking to media with Jonathan Huston in the background.

items.title: ASX down as ACCC announces anti-price gouging law

children.content: The ASX has slipped into negative territory after lunch on Friday. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

items.title: A failed forest utopia is watching nervously as the Socceroos play Paraguay

items.title: Queensland to introduce new drink and drug driving laws to fix 'loophole'

children.content: The Crisafulli government has announced a crackdown on dangerous driving while doubling down on a "zero-tolerance" approach to drug driving, including for medical cannabis users.

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

children.content: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

items.title: King Charles won't move into Buckingham palace, breaking royal tradition

items.title: Barring 'ISIS bride' could have triggered High Court challenge

items.title: Terminally ill 72-year-old still chasing solo sailing dreams

children.content: David Burston had only just bought an old yacht to fix up and sail around Australia when he was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Hee has decided to keep pursuing his dream for as long as he can.

items.title: Mixed reaction to beach town at forefront of over-50s lifestyle resort boom

children.content: Four over-50s lifestyle developments have been approved for this small beach community, with two more being assessed. Some worry the area is not ready for the influx, others say it is the answer to the housing crisis.

items.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation

children.content: Victoria Police close an investigation into allegations state MP Matthew Guy assaulted fellow Liberal Moira Deeming at a community event, determining there was "there was no offence detected".

image.altText: A man in a suit stands looking sad.

items.title: New bridge announced but Great Western Highway closed for another year

children.content: A new bridge will be built at Mitchell's Causeway on the Great Western Highway to link Sydney and the state's central west.

items.title: 'Not everyone wants to go for a run': Why young adults joined rubbish club

children.content: From recently single, to expats, paid and free clubs designed to help adults make friends are attracting people from all walks of life. As Australians increasingly report feeling lonely, these are the people who are branching out to change that.
